rationale: The citing paper uses Keller, Geyskens, and Dekimpe (2020) as more than a passing mention: it is cited to support a specific empirical regularity (price elasticity becomes less negative after rebranding) and to frame prior work on marketing conduct/effectiveness under a rebranding lens. This helps motivate the paper's conceptual setup for why a soda tax may shift marketing effectiveness, not just prices. At the same time, the citing study does not directly reuse the cited paper's private-label umbrella branding framework or test its core mechanism, so the link is substantive but not a direct extend/refine/challenge relation.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on the cited paper as prior evidence that a discrete market event (rebranding) can change marketing-mix effectiveness, especially price sensitivity. It then contrasts this prior rebranding lens with its own policy-shock context (soda taxes) to motivate its research framing.
